auth.rememberPasswordHint: Use "Remember password" only on your private device.

users.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this user?

llm.title: LLM Configuration
llm.testTimeout: Connection test timed out. Check model, base URL, or network.
llm.taskRoutesHint: Assign different models to the interactive learning loop, heavier structured generation, and non-stream fallback.

routeDescriptions.interactive: Exploration answers, Socratic prompts, step hints, and structured feedback in the main workspace.
routeDescriptions.structured_heavy: Concept extraction, learning path generation, model card creation, and other heavier structured artifacts.
routeDescriptions.fallback: Second attempt for non-stream tasks when the primary lane returns an error or empty result.

llm.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this?
llm.createFirst: Add your first provider to get started

dashboard.focusSubtitle: Put the next meaningful learning step first, and keep metrics and tools in the background.
dashboard.quickActionTitle: Jump into the flows you use most
dashboard.momentumDescription: A compact view of whether your learning is moving forward or stalling out.
dashboard.reviewQueueReady: You have {count} reviews ready to clear
dashboard.reviewQueueDescription: Clearing due reviews before new work keeps the learning loop steady.
dashboard.resumeLatestProblemDescription: If the next move is unclear, push one concrete problem forward first.
dashboard.modelLibraryDescription: Capture stable understanding into model cards so review and transfer have real material to work with.
dashboard.exploreInWorkspaceDescription: Ask inside ProblemDetail for "{title}" so exploration stays tied to the learning path.
dashboard.exploreInWorkspaceFallback: Start from a problem so concept exploration stays attached to a real learning turn.
dashboard.reviewRecommendation: Recommended now: generate a {type} review for {period}
dashboard.errorMessage: The dashboard could not load. Retry to restore the current learning focus.
dashboard.retrievalDescription: A compact view of what context retrieval is pulling in, so retrieval stops being a black box.
dashboard.noRetrievalHits: No context hits for this event

problems.title: Problems
problems.subtitle: Choose a problem and enter one learning protocol. This page should help you resume or start work quickly, not manage downstream assets.
problems.filtersHint: Use these only when you need to narrow the library. The default view should help you resume a problem fast.
problems.createFirst: Create your first problem to start learning
problems.createTimeout: Problem creation timed out. Please try again.
problems.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this problem?
problems.errorMessage: The problem list could not load. Retry to restore your learning queue.
problems.startModeMessage: Choose the learning protocol before you create the problem and enter the workspace.

problemDetail.title: Problem Details
problemDetail.workspaceFrameSocratic: You are in an evaluated learning turn. Answer the current step, inspect the turn result, and decide what should move forward.
problemDetail.workspaceFrameExploration: You are in an exploration turn. Ask about the current concept, inspect derived concepts and path options, and decide what to pursue next.
problemDetail.noLearningPath: Learning path is not available yet
problemDetail.progressInputLabel: Your understanding and attempts at this step
problemDetail.progressInputPlaceholder: Write what you understood, what you tried, and where you got stuck
problemDetail.completedAll: All steps are completed. Review your history and consolidate a model card.
problemDetail.modeSocraticHint: Respond to the current step and let the system evaluate whether you should progress.
problemDetail.modeExplorationHint: Ask about a concept in the current step and use the answer to discover related ideas.
problemDetail.workspaceOverviewSubtitle: Keep the current focus, path position, learning mode, latest turn output, and next move visible in one battleground.
problemDetail.exportLearningFailed: The learning record could not be exported. Retry from the workspace.
problemDetail.workspaceAssetsSubtitle: Open only when you want supporting material beyond the current learning move.
problemDetail.reinforcementPathHint: Resume the exact learning branch when weak recall came from a side path.
problemDetail.reinforcementResumeHint: Use this as the re-entry point before pushing the path further.
problemDetail.reinforcementFocusCandidate: Start with this derived concept artifact. Current status: {status}. Source: {source}.
problemDetail.reinforcementFocusTurn: Start from the source learning turn in the workspace. It was produced as a {answerType} turn.
problemDetail.reinforcementFocusFallback: Start by restating the weak concept in the highlighted workspace artifact before moving on.
problemDetail.reinforcementComparisonLikelyConfusion: Treating {cue} as if both sides of the tradeoff move together.
problemDetail.reinforcementBoundaryLikelyConfusion: Overextending the earlier claim "{cue}" without checking the edge case.
problemDetail.reinforcementMisconceptionLikelyConfusio: Leaning on the earlier claim "{cue}" instead of restating the corrected version.
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplateRestateTitle: Restate it in your own words
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplateRestateBody: Write a short restatement of {concept} before you continue with {anchor}.
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplateRestateSourceStarte:
1. In the earlier turn about {cue}, {concept} means ...
2. The shaky part was ...
3. I would now restate it as ...
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplateCompareBody: Write a compact contrast between {concept} and {anchor} before moving on.
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplateCompareStarter:
1. {concept} is different from {anchor} because ...
2. I would choose one over the other when ...
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplateCompareSourceStarte:
1. In the earlier comparison about {cue}, {concept} changes because ...
2. The contrast I should check first is ...
3. I know I rebuilt it when I can explain the tradeoff cleanly.
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplateCorrectBody: State the wrong version first, then replace it with the corrected version of {concept}.
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplateCorrectStarter:
1. The incorrect version of {concept} is ...
2. The correction is ...
3. I know this because ...
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplateCorrectSourceStarte:
1. In the earlier confusion about {cue}, the tempting but wrong move is ...
2. The correction for {concept} is ...
3. I would test that correction by ...
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplateExampleBody: Use one small example to rebuild {concept} before applying it back to {anchor}.
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplateExampleStarter:
1. A minimal example of {concept} is ...
2. It works because ...
3. In {anchor}, this changes ...
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplateExampleSourceStarte:
1. Use {cue} as the minimal case for {concept}: ...
2. It works because ...
3. I know the example is solid when ...
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplateBoundaryBody: Clarify where {concept} applies and where it breaks before you continue.
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplateBoundaryStarter:
1. {concept} applies when ...
2. It stops applying when ...
3. One edge case is ...
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplateBoundarySourceStart:
1. In the earlier turn about {cue}, {concept} applies when ...
2. It stops applying when ...
3. One edge case I should check is ...
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplatePrerequisiteBody: Name the prerequisite behind {concept} before pushing it further into {anchor}.
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplatePrerequisiteStarter:
1. {concept} depends on ...
2. Without that prerequisite, I get stuck because ...
3. In {anchor}, that prerequisite matters because ...
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplatePrerequisiteSourceS:
1. Before I can use {concept} in {cue}, I need ...
2. Without that prerequisite, I get stuck because ...
3. The first sign the prerequisite is missing is ...
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplateProbeBody: Give one compact answer that re-stabilizes {concept} before continuing with {anchor}.
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplateProbeStarter:
1. {concept} means ...
2. The key evidence is ...
3. In {anchor}, I would use it when ...
problemDetail.reinforcementTemplateProbeSourceStarter:
1. For the earlier turn about {cue}, {concept} means ...
2. The key evidence is ...
3. I would check it by ...
problemDetail.reinforcementActionHint: Treat this as the immediate follow-up from weak recall.
problemDetail.reinforcementResumeCurrentWorkspace: Resume in the current workspace context
problemDetail.workspaceArtifactsSubtitle: Keep this turn's output in front. Older concept and path artifacts stay tucked below.
problemDetail.workspaceArtifactConceptCountHint: Current-turn concept candidates stay visible first.
problemDetail.workspaceArtifactPathCountHint: Current-turn path options stay visible first.
problemDetail.turnResultSubtitleSocratic: See whether this answer advanced mastery, created concepts, or opened new paths.
problemDetail.turnResultSubtitleExploration: See what this exploration turn explained, derived, and suggested next.
problemDetail.noTurnResultSocratic: Submit a Socratic answer to see mastery and next-step results here.
problemDetail.noTurnResultExploration: Ask an exploration question to see explanation artifacts here.
problemDetail.askSubtitle: You can ask questions at any time during learning, and the answer is grounded in the current step.
problemDetail.askInputPlaceholder: For example: Why should we use F1 instead of only accuracy on imbalanced data?
problemDetail.askStreamingHint: The workspace is drafting the explanation before it derives concepts and next actions.
problemDetail.socraticStreamEvaluating: Assessing mastery, misconceptions, and the next Socratic move.
problemDetail.socraticStreamExtracting: Deriving concept and path artifacts from this answer.
problemDetail.socraticStreamSaving: Saving the evaluated turn and updating the workspace state.
problemDetail.conceptGovernanceSubtitle: Review, confirm, or rollback concept updates before they affect your long-term knowledge map.
problemDetail.derivedConceptsSubtitle: Review concept candidates in the same learning turn where they appeared, and decide whether they belong in your knowledge model.
problemDetail.derivedConceptSourceResponse: Derived from your answer in this step
problemDetail.derivedConceptSourceAsk: Derived from your exploration question in this step
problemDetail.sourceTurnUnavailable: This candidate was created earlier and the turn preview is unavailable.
problemDetail.handoffHintPromoteConcepts: Promote accepted concepts into model cards from the derived concepts panel.
problemDetail.handoffHintReviewPendingConcepts: Review pending concept candidates before they become long-term knowledge.
problemDetail.handoffHintReviewPathSuggestions: Decide how to place the suggested learning paths below.
problemDetail.pathCandidatesSubtitle: Turns can suggest prerequisite, comparison, or deep-dive paths before you decide how to place them.
problemDetail.workspaceReviewEmpty: No concepts from this workspace are in recall yet.
problemDetail.workspaceReviewScheduled: {count} concepts from this workspace are in recall
problemDetail.workspaceReviewStable: {count} concepts are holding steady in recall
problemDetail.workspaceReviewEmptyHint: Promote accepted concepts and schedule recall once they are stable enough to reinforce.
problemDetail.workspaceReviewPathsPending: {count} path follow-up options still need placement.
problemDetail.workspaceNextSocraticDefault: Answer the current Socratic question to check mastery and progression.
problemDetail.workspaceNextExplorationDefault: Ask about the current concept to derive explanations, concepts, and next steps.
problemDetail.workspaceNextReviewRevisit: Recall weakened on {concept}. Revisit it in this workspace before pushing further.
problemDetail.workspaceNextReviewReinforce: Recall on {concept} held with effort. Reinforce it once more in the workspace.
problemDetail.workspaceNextReviewExtend: {concept} is holding in recall. Use the pending path options to extend or compare it.
problemDetail.workspaceNextPrerequisiteBranch: Close the gap on '{concept}', then return to step {step} on the main path for '{target}'.
problemDetail.reviewActionCompleteFirstRecall: Complete the first recall when it comes due.
problemDetail.reviewActionRevisitWorkspace: Return to the workspace and restate or correct it before moving on.
problemDetail.reviewActionReinforceSoon: Run another short recall soon to lock it in.
problemDetail.reviewActionKeepSpacing: Keep spacing reviews while continuing the current learning path.
problemDetail.reviewActionExtendOrCompare: Use this as a stable base for comparison or branch expansion.
problemDetail.guidedLine3: What I am still unsure about:
problemDetail.autoAdvanced: This step is considered mastered and has been advanced automatically.
problemDetail.autoAdvanceUndone: Auto-advance was undone. You can keep refining this step.
problemDetail.currentOutputEmptyTitle: No output in this mode yet
problemDetail.currentOutputEmptyDescriptionSocratic: This mode has no current-turn output yet. Answer the current question to create a fresh evaluated turn.
problemDetail.currentOutputEmptyDescriptionExploration: This mode has no current-turn output yet. Ask a question to generate concepts, paths, and next-step suggestions.
problemDetail.socraticProgressControlled: Step progression in Socratic mode is decided by the evaluation result, not by manual advance.
problemDetail.errorMessage: The workspace could not load. Retry to restore the current problem.
problemDetail.actionErrorModeSwitch: The learning mode could not be changed. Retry the switch.
problemDetail.actionErrorFetchQuestion: The next Socratic question could not be loaded. Retry the step action.
problemDetail.actionErrorSubmitResponse: The Socratic answer could not be submitted. Your current draft is still here.
problemDetail.actionErrorAskQuestion: The exploration question could not be submitted. Retry when ready.
problemDetail.actionErrorStepChange: The current step could not be updated. Retry the step change.
problemDetail.actionErrorPathSwitch: The learning path could not be switched. Retry the path change.
problemDetail.actionErrorReturnPath: The workspace could not return to the parent path. Retry the return action.
problemDetail.contractSubtitle: Keep the current task, the reason for doing it now, and the completion bar visible before you act.
problemDetail.contractDonePrerequisite: Close this prerequisite gap, then return to step {step} on {target}.
problemDetail.contractDoneExploration: Leave this turn with a clearer explanation and one concrete next learning move.
problemDetail.contractDoneSocratic: Answer clearly enough for the system to judge whether this step should advance.
problemDetail.contractDoneSupportCompleted: This path is already complete. Use the result and follow-up decisions below to choose whether to branch, consolidate, or stop.
problemDetail.contractDoneSupportPrerequisite: Use this branch only to close the missing idea. Do not keep extending it after the return condition is met.
problemDetail.contractDoneSupportExploration: Keep the question narrow enough that one answer can clarify the step and unlock one concrete next move.
problemDetail.contractDoneSupportSocratic: Give a concrete explanation or example so the evaluation can judge this step instead of only restating a definition.
problemDetail.postTurnHeading: Decide what to do with this turn
problemDetail.postTurnSubtitle: Treat concepts and path suggestions as follow-up decisions after you have read the result.
problemDetail.postTurnConceptSummary: Process concept candidates after you understand this turn.
problemDetail.postTurnPathSummary: Place path suggestions only after the current result is clear.
problemDetail.secondaryToolsSubtitle: Keep these available without letting them interrupt the current learning turn.
problemDetail.pathControlsSubtitle: Use these only when you need to change where you are learning. They are secondary to the current turn.

modelCards.title: Model Cards
modelCards.subtitle: Model cards are durable knowledge assets. Refine them here, keep their origin traceable, and send ready cards into spaced review.
modelCards.draftCardSummary: This draft is not in review yet. Tighten the core note and seed examples before you schedule it.
modelCards.draftNeedsActivation: Drafts stay outside spaced review until you mark them ready.
modelCards.newDraftHelper: Start with a durable wording and one or two example seeds. You can mark it ready for review after a quick pass.
modelCards.draftDetailSummary: Use this draft to shape a durable knowledge asset before it enters the review lifecycle.
modelCards.revisionCounterExamplesPlaceholder: e.g., fails when threshold is fixed too early
modelCards.evolutionStateNeedsRevisionSummary: Weak recall sent this card back to {workspace}. Rebuild it before extending the card.
modelCards.evolutionStateRebuildingSummary: Recall held with effort. Keep rebuilding it in {workspace} before treating it as stable.
modelCards.evolutionStateReinforcedRecentlySummary: Recent recalls are holding. Keep spacing it while you continue nearby work.
modelCards.evolutionStateStableBaseSummary: Recent recall is strong enough to use this card as a stable base.
modelCards.evolutionStateFirstRecallQueuedSummary: This card is queued for its first recall.
modelCards.evolutionStateRepeatedConfusionSummary: This card has re-entered reinforcement {count} times. Keep it narrow and correct in {workspace} before extending it.
modelCards.revisionFocusReviseNotesSummary: Tighten the core wording for this card in {workspace} before you add more detail.
modelCards.revisionFocusRevisitExampleSummary: Use one concrete example from {workspace} to restabilize this card before extending it.
modelCards.revisionFocusClarifyBoundarySummary: Clarify where this card applies and where it breaks in {workspace} before treating it as stable.
modelCards.revisionFocusAddCounterExampleSummary: Add one counter-example from {workspace} so the card stops overgeneralizing.
modelCards.revisionFocusRevisitComparisonSummary: Rework the comparison or tradeoff from {workspace} before extending this card.
modelCards.recallActionCompleteFirstRecall: Complete the first recall when it comes due.
modelCards.recallActionRevisitWorkspace: Revisit the origin workspace before extending this card.
modelCards.recallActionReinforceSoon: Reinforce it again soon before treating it as stable.
modelCards.recallActionKeepSpacing: Keep it in spaced recall while continuing nearby work.
modelCards.recallActionExtendOrCompare: Use it as a stable base for transfer, comparison, or branching.
modelCards.reinforcementResumeCurrentCard: Rebuild this concept before extending the card
modelCards.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this model card?

practice.title: Practice
practice.secondaryHeading: Use practice as a supporting drill surface, not as the main learning loop
practice.secondaryMessage: ProblemDetail, Model Cards, and Reviews carry the main cognition-building flow. Practice remains available as a supporting place to rehearse or spot-check understanding.
practice.checkBack: Check back later for new challenges

reviews.title: Reviews
reviews.subtitle: Clear due recall first, then return to the workspace when a concept needs reinforcement.
reviews.focusModelCards: Recent model cards are ready to refine
reviews.focusEmptyDescription: No due recall and no reinforcement target are waiting. Return to a problem and keep the learning loop moving.
reviews.reviewQueueHint: Start here when recall is due. Archive and card editing stay secondary until the queue is clear.
reviews.originModeExploration: Entered recall from an exploration-derived concept.
reviews.originModeSocratic: Entered recall after a Socratic learning turn.
reviews.originModeUnknown: Entered recall from a linked learning outcome.
reviews.modelLifecycleHint: Use these entries to decide what to rebuild in context before you keep expanding the knowledge asset.
reviews.errorMessage: The review hub could not load. Retry to restore the recall queue and model-card context.
reviews.archiveHint: Archive and manually generated reviews stay secondary. Use them after the current recall decision is clear.
reviews.modelLifecycleSourceNone: No workspace source is linked yet.
reviews.modelLifecycleStateScheduled: Scheduled and waiting for its next recall.
reviews.modelLifecycleStateReviewed: Already reviewed and staying in recall.
reviews.modelLifecycleActionSchedule: Open the card and schedule its first recall.
reviews.modelLifecycleActionOpen: Open the card and inspect its current recall status.
reviews.modelLifecycleActionRecallNow: Start recall now before opening archive work.
reviews.modelLifecycleActionRevisit: Open the originating workspace or card and rebuild it before extending.

chat.title: Legacy Conversations
chat.selectConversation: Select a legacy conversation or start a new one
chat.legacyTitle: Standalone chat is outside the primary learning loop
chat.legacyMessage: Use ProblemDetail exploration for concept questions you want attached to a problem, its learning turns, and its derived paths. This page remains available only as a legacy conversation surface.

srs.title: Recall Session
srs.secondaryHeading: Use this page as a focused review session inside the broader review loop
srs.secondaryMessage: This page handles the active recall session itself. The broader planning, scheduling, and evolution loop still lives in Reviews and Model Cards.
srs.recallPrompt: Try to recall the key concepts of this model card
srs.originModeExploration: This card entered recall from an exploration-derived concept in the learning workspace.
srs.originModeSocratic: This card entered recall after a Socratic learning turn in the workspace.
srs.originModeUnknown: This card entered recall from a linked learning outcome.
srs.recallActionCompleteFirstRecall: Complete the first recall when it comes due.
srs.recallActionRevisitWorkspace: Return to the workspace and repair the concept before moving on.
srs.recallActionReinforceSoon: Run another recall soon to stabilize it.
srs.recallActionKeepSpacing: Keep spacing recalls while continuing nearby learning.
srs.recallActionExtendOrCompare: Use this stable concept for extension or comparison work.
srs.reinforcementResumeCurrentWorkspace: Return to the linked workspace context
srs.errorMessage: The review queue could not load. Retry to restore due cards.
srs.submitErrorMessage: This recall result could not be submitted. The current card is still ready for rating.

graph.title: Knowledge Graph
graph.secondaryHeading: Use this to inspect structure, not to drive the main loop
graph.secondaryMessage: The primary learning loop stays in Problems, Review, and Model Cards. Use the graph only when you need a structural view across those assets.
graph.noData: No model cards to visualize yet

challenges.title: Cognitive Challenges
challenges.secondaryHeading: Use this as an occasional stress test, not as the default review flow
challenges.secondaryMessage: Main review and consolidation now live in the Review hub and ProblemDetail. Challenges remain available as a secondary way to probe edge cases.
challenges.noChallenges: No challenges yet. Generate one to get started!

resources.title: Reference Archive
resources.secondaryHeading: Use this archive for supporting material, not as the primary learning workspace
resources.secondaryMessage: The core loop lives in Problems, ProblemDetail, Model Cards, and Reviews. Keep new supporting references attached there whenever possible.
resources.archiveSubtitle: Primary reference capture now lives inside ProblemDetail. This page is a secondary archive for general and problem-linked supporting material.
resources.urlPlaceholder: Enter a supporting article or video...
resources.noResources: No reference material yet. Add one to get started.
resources.workspaceSubtitle: Attach reference material to this problem so explanations and next actions stay grounded in supporting context.
resources.currentTurnContext: This reference is attached to the current turn and this problem.
resources.problemOnlyContext: This reference is attached to this problem.
resources.noProblemResources: No references attached to this problem yet.

notes.title: Annotation Archive
notes.secondaryHeading: Use this archive for annotations, not as the primary product surface
notes.secondaryMessage: The main learning activity belongs in ProblemDetail. Use this page only to revisit supporting annotations that were captured around problems and turns.
notes.archiveSubtitle: Primary annotation capture now lives inside ProblemDetail. This page is a secondary archive for general and problem-linked learning annotations.
notes.placeholder: Capture a short observation, misconception, or reminder...
notes.voiceNotSupported: Your browser does not support speech recognition
notes.noNotes: No annotations yet. Capture the first supporting note.
notes.workspaceSubtitle: Capture supporting context for this problem and, when available, for the current learning turn.
notes.currentTurnContext: This note is attached to the current turn and this problem.
notes.problemOnlyContext: This note is attached to this problem.
notes.noProblemNotes: No annotations attached to this problem yet.

cogTest.secondaryHeading: Use this for diagnosis, not for the main learning loop
cogTest.secondaryMessage: Cognitive tests stay available as a deeper diagnostic tool, but model cards and reviews remain the primary path for consolidation and evolution.
